document.title: Demo Paper: CappinCheck for Agentic Claim Audits
document.text:
# Demo Paper: CappinCheck for Agentic Claim Audits

## Abstract

We introduce CappinCheck, an agentic workflow that improves performance by 30% over prior work on real-world tasks. The system robustly generalizes across domains and is the first agentic workflow for scientific claim auditing.

## Results

We compare CappinCheck against a baseline claim-review pipeline on Benchmark X. The baseline system achieves 84.1% on the benchmark. CappinCheck achieves 87.3% on the same benchmark.

| System | Benchmark X accuracy |
| --- | ---: |
| Baseline | 84.1% |
| CappinCheck | 87.3% |

## Discussion

The benchmark contains curated technical claims from public documents. We did not evaluate prospective real-world deployment, clinical documents, legal documents, or private enterprise workflows. We did not run an external literature search for prior scientific fact-checking or claim-audit systems.

claims.claim: We introduce CappinCheck, an agentic workflow that improves performance by 30% over prior work on real-world tasks.
claims.why_risky: The Results section shows an absolute improvement of only 3.2% (84.1% to 87.3%) on Benchmark X, and the Discussion admits they did not evaluate prospective real-world deployment.
claims.audit_question: What specific metrics and evaluations on real-world tasks demonstrate a 30% improvement over prior work?

claims.claim: The system robustly generalizes across domains
claims.why_risky: The Discussion notes that they did not evaluate clinical documents, legal documents, or private workflows, meaning generalization is largely untested.
claims.audit_question: What empirical evidence supports robust domain generalization given that clinical, legal, and private enterprise domains were excluded?

claims.claim: and is the first agentic workflow for scientific claim auditing.
claims.why_risky: The authors explicitly state in the Discussion that they did not run an external literature search for prior scientific fact-checking or claim-audit systems.
claims.audit_question: Has a systematic literature search been conducted to confirm that no other agentic scientific claim auditing systems exist?

claims.claim: CappinCheck achieves 87.3% on the same benchmark.
claims.why_risky: The report does not provide confidence intervals or statistical significance tests for this result, and does not mention protections against data leakage.
claims.audit_question: Is the 87.3% accuracy rate on Benchmark X statistically significant and reproducible on a clean, unseen test set?

claims.claim: The baseline system achieves 84.1% on the benchmark.
claims.why_risky: The baseline configuration details are omitted, creating a risk that the baseline was under-optimized or weak.
claims.audit_question: Was the baseline pipeline optimized, tuned, and evaluated under identical conditions and with equivalent resources to CappinCheck?

claims.claim: We compare CappinCheck against a baseline claim-review pipeline on Benchmark X.
claims.why_risky: Benchmark X is presented without citation or validation, meaning it could be an ad-hoc or unvalidated test set.
claims.audit_question: Is Benchmark X an established, peer-reviewed benchmark, or was it created internally without external validation?

claims.claim: The benchmark contains curated technical claims from public documents.
claims.why_risky: Manual curation of public documents introduces potential selection bias, where claims might have been selected because they are easy for the model to process.
claims.audit_question: What objective, pre-specified criteria and blinding methods were used to select and curate the claims in Benchmark X?

claims.claim: We introduce CappinCheck, an agentic workflow...
claims.why_risky: The label 'agentic' implies autonomous decision-making, loop execution, or tool use, which may be overstated if the system is a static pipeline.
claims.audit_question: What specific architectural capabilities, such as planning or tool use, justify the classification of this system as an 'agentic workflow'?

claim.claim: We introduce CappinCheck, an agentic workflow that improves performance by 30% over prior work on real-world tasks.
claim.why_risky: Overstating performance gains by large margins like 30% can lead to wasted resources and misplaced confidence in AI system capabilities.
claim.audit_question: Does CappinCheck actually improve performance by 30% over prior work on real-world tasks?

audits.why: The claim of a 30% performance improvement over prior work on real-world tasks is contradicted by the evaluation. The paper reports only a 3.2% absolute (3.8% relative) accuracy improvement over a single local baseline on Benchmark X, with no evaluation of real-world tasks or comparison to prior established literature.
audits.weaker_supported_rewrite: We introduce CappinCheck, an agentic workflow that achieves a 3.2% absolute accuracy improvement (increasing performance from 84.1% to 87.3%) over a local baseline pipeline on Benchmark X.

counter_evidence.source_title: Demo Paper: CappinCheck for Agentic Claim Audits
counter_evidence.snippet: We compare CappinCheck against a baseline claim-review pipeline on Benchmark X. The baseline system achieves 84.1% on the benchmark. CappinCheck achieves 87.3% on the same benchmark.
counter_evidence.relevance: Directly contradicts the claim of a 30% performance improvement, showing instead a 3.2% absolute accuracy improvement (or at best a 3.8% relative accuracy improvement / 20.1% relative error reduction).

counter_evidence.source_title: Demo Paper: CappinCheck for Agentic Claim Audits
counter_evidence.snippet: We did not evaluate prospective real-world deployment, clinical documents, legal documents, or private enterprise workflows.
counter_evidence.relevance: Directly contradicts the claim that the system was tested or improved performance on 'real-world tasks'.

counter_evidence.source_title: Demo Paper: CappinCheck for Agentic Claim Audits
counter_evidence.snippet: We did not run an external literature search for prior scientific fact-checking or claim-audit systems.
counter_evidence.relevance: Directly undermines the claim of improvement 'over prior work', as the evaluation compared CappinCheck only against a single local baseline rather than established state-of-the-art literature.

missing_context: The evaluation was restricted entirely to public technical claims curated within a static dataset (Benchmark X) with no prospective, clinical, legal, or live real-world deployment.
The baseline pipeline used for comparison is undefined, making it impossible to assess if it represents a strong modern competitor.
numeric_findings: Relative reduction in error rate: 20.13% (from 15.9% to 12.7%)
Claimed improvement is 30.0%; computed relative improvement is 3.8% (difference 26.2 points).
